tweets.text:
Not everyone online is who they say they are. 🚨

Kendall Carter is serving 30 years in federal prison for victimizing more than a dozen teens and children.

His case from @DHSgov’s national public awareness campaign, @Know2Protect, is a powerful reminder: Offenders often use fake profiles to manipulate and exploit kids online.

Help young people spot #FakeProfiles before harm happens using the Tips2Identify Fake Profiles resource at https://t.co/m7m0YvigvJ #K2PAnniversary

tweets.text:
Enrique Garcia-Pineda, a criminal illegal alien from Mexico, was sentenced to 16 years in prison for LEWD OR LASCIVIOUS ACTS WITH A MINOR UNDER 16 in Gooding County, ID, in 2003.

Despite a 2016 illegal entry conviction, ICE @EROSeattle Fugitive Operations officers in Idaho arrested him May 7. 

He faces deportation for:
⚖️INA § 237(a)(2)(A)(iii): Aggravated felony
⚖️INA § 237(a)(2)(E)(i): Stalking and child abuse
⚖️INA § 212(a)(6)(A)(i): Alien present without admission or parole
⚖️INA § 212(a)(9)(C)(iii) Previously ordered removed and entered or attempted to enter without being admitted

Nearly two dozen members of the La Familia Nunca Muere ("The Family Never Dies") criminal organization were charged with racketeering and murder thanks to a Homeland Security Task Force initiative.

14 of the defendants, if convicted, may be eligible for the death penalty.

tweets.text:
Sunny Manhertz, of Canada, who is a lawful permanent resident and therefore ineligible to vote in federal elections, was arrested and charged for allegedly voting in federal elections since 2008.
 
Manhertz made an initial appearance in federal court in Boston today.
 
Acting Special Agent in Charge of ICE @HSINewEngland Jeff Grimming and @DMAnews1 made the announcement today.
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
🚨UPDATE: Police disclosed more information about their arrest of 21-year-old Guatemalan illegal alien Henry Roldan Perez-Roblero. 

Perez-Roblero faces a felony charge in Florida for allegedly sexually assaulting a child under 12 — a capital felony punishable in the state by death.

ICE issued a detainer with law enforcement in Martin County, Florida, and will take custody of him if he is ever released.

➡️Under @POTUS @realDonaldTrump and @SecMullinDHS, if you break the law, you will face the consequences. Criminal illegal aliens are not welcome in the U.S.

tweets.text:
ICE @EROBuffalo arrested suspected MS-13 gang member and Salvadoran illegal alien Josue Coreas-Chavez when he was released from Wyoming Correctional Facility in Attica, NY.  

He was sentenced for burglary, conspiracy and criminal facilitation. He’s also got pending charges for robbery, kidnapping, assault, coercion, unlawful imprisonment and grand larceny.

He’ll remain in custody pending a deportation decision.

tweets.text:
ICE @HSI_HQ Springfield investigated Brayanne Escobar-Guarnizo, of Colombia, with the Green County Sheriff’s Office.

🔎Escobar entered the U.S. illegally in 2023, under the Biden administration, with his wife and two children. He’s currently in removal proceedings, awaiting sentencing for a child exploitation conviction and facing child rape charges.

ICE is prepared to escort him home to Colombia after he faces justice in court.

tweets.text:
Jose Luis Mata-Flores, of Mexico, was convicted of second-degree murder with a firearm Jan. 21, 1981, in Los Angeles. 

⚖️He was sentenced to up to life in prison, with a minimum of 15 years.

ICE arrested him upon his release from prison and removed him to his home nation May 4. https://t.co/CiXQquRACL

tweets.text:
👟In 2018, Antonio Efrain Morales-Diego, of Guatemala, told Border Patrol agents that he snuck into the U.S. on foot near San Luis, Ariz. 

➡️Border Patrol released him on an order of recognizance and issued him a notice to appear.

Morales-Diego made his way to Virginia where he was later convicted of abduction and strangulation.

ICE arrested him upon his release from a Virginia correctional facility based on:
⚖️INA § 237(a)(2)(A)(iii): Alien convicted of an aggravated felony

tweets.text:
Zakaria Songotoua was convicted in absentia and sentenced to 30 years for murder, assault and battery after he fled Burkina Faso before trial. 

As a member of the Presidential Security Regiment, he participated in the attempted 2015 coup d’etat in the African nation that included killing 11 and holding the interim president hostage.

After hiding in the sanctuary city of Bronx, N.Y., our New York officers and special agents tracked him down and returned him to Burkina Faso to face justice.

The U.S. is no safe haven for war criminals, and ICE HSI's Human Rights Violators and War Crimes Center ensures those who engage in such bloodshed anywhere in the world have no place in the United States.

tweets.text:
Brenda Liliana Rivera-Estrada was arrested by police in Arizona on charges of manslaughter and child abuse, but Maricopa county prosecutors declined to file charges.

ICE officers picked her up pursuant to an honored detainer, and she will be held in ICE detention pending a review of her case by the DOJ’s Executive Office for Immigration Review.

Read more⬇️

tweets.text:
Five defendants pleaded guilty to federal firearms offenses while a federal grand jury indicted eight others in an international firearms trafficking conspiracy that funneled illegally obtained weapons from New Hampshire through the Akwesasne Mohawk Indian Reservation in New York.

ICE @HSIBuffalo, NY, and @ATFHQ led the investigation that identified about 50 firearms potentially trafficked from New Hampshire to Canada through Vermont and New York, several of which were recovered at various crime scenes, to include kidnapping and attempted murder, in Canada.

tweets.text:
Vicente Margarito Lopez-Sixto was convicted of paying to sexually assault a child after being arrested by police in Woodburn, Oregon, Aug. 17, 2017. Six years later, he was convicted of stalking in the same city.

🚨ICE arrested him near his home in Woodburn May 7 during a targeted immigration enforcement operation.

author.description: Protects America through criminal investigations and by enforcing immigration law to preserve national security and public safety.  https://t.co/rDI2IeFofc

tweets.text:
ICE’s first interaction with Alejandro Paulino-Espidio was in 2006, when he was found among 17 illegal aliens hiding in the back of a van. He was granted voluntary return to Mexico, but that wasn’t the last ICE would see of him.

He snuck back into the United States by climbing a border fence in 2018. When Border Patrol agents found him, he claimed he planned to live with some friends in Orange County, California. Federal officials deported him that time.

But he returned illegally — again. 

Then, in 2022, a California judge in Fresno County convicted him of arson. A few years later, a California judge in Merced County convicted him of escaping from custody and vandalism.

➡️ICE deported Paulino-Espidio May 16. His repeated disregard of Americans boundaries, laws and safety shows clearly why @DHSgov has prioritized immigration enforcement and removal AND border security.

tweets.text:
🔎Fredis Antonio Gonzalez-Recinos had a green card, but threw it away by sexually abusing a child younger than 13 when he was over 21-years-old. He pleaded guilty in Suffolk County, N.Y., of the crime last year and was sentenced to 10 years probation.

🚨ICE Fugitive Operations officers arrested him May 8 near his last known residence in Mastic, N.Y., during a targeted immigration enforcement operation.

Protects America through criminal investigations and by enforcing immigration law to preserve national security and public safety.  https://t.co/rDI2IeFofc

tweets.text:
Irving, Texas, police charged Magdaleno Roman-Rosales, of Mexico, with kidnapping many years ago and he ultimately received a 120-day sentence for unlawful restraint. He was subsequently removed to his home country. At some point, he returned to the United States illegally. 

🔎ICE @ERODallas Fugitive Operations arrested him during a targeted immigration enforcement operation near his residence May 8. During the arrest, he put our officers, the public and himself in danger by attempting to flee.
 
🚨He backed his truck into an ICE vehicle. Before he was able to drive away, skilled ICE officers were able to gain entry into the vehicle. Then, Roman-Rosales refused to exit his truck and had to be physically removed from the cab. He continued to resist his arrest at every step. Thankfully, no one was seriously injured during the operation. 

➡️He will stay in ICE custody pending prosecution.
